rules.name: block-git-checkout-ref-path
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-checkout-file-restore
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-rm
rules.reason: STOP NOW. rm is BANNED. NEVER use rm. ALWAYS use gio trash for file removal. First git add + git commit to checkpoint, then gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm), then commit the trashed state.

rules.name: block-find-delete
rules.reason: STOP NOW. find -delete is BANNED (same as rm). NEVER use find -delete. ALWAYS use gio trash. First git add + git commit to checkpoint, then gio trash the file, then commit the trashed state.

rules.name: block-trash-empty
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Emptying trash is BANNED. Permanent deletions must be user actions only. Agents may not permanently delete any files from the system.

rules.name: block-sed
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Direct file manipulation with sed is BANNED. Use proper editing tools (Write, Edit) so changes produce clear diffs for user review in chat.

rules.name: block-git-switch
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-branch-delete
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not delete branches — local or remote. Branch management is user-controlled. If a branch is no longer needed, leave it in place and notify the user.

rules.name: block-git-branch-manipulate
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not rename, copy, or otherwise manipulate branches. Branch management is user-controlled.

rules.name: block-git-add-all
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Blind git add -A / git add . may commit unintended files (secrets, build artifacts, large binaries). Agents must use targeted git add <path> for each file, never blanket staging. Use git status and git diff to identify specific files, then add them individually.

rules.name: block-git-add-interactive
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Interactive staging requires a TTY and is not supported in headless agent environments. Agents must use targeted git add <path> instead.

rules.name: block-git-stash
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-stash-pop
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-revert
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-reset

If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-clean

If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-restore

If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-rebase
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-merge
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. Agents do not discard or overwrite user working state. The current state is user-owned. If a file should not exist: (1) git add + git commit the current state (checkpoint everything), (2) gio trash the file (NOT rm, NOT git rm, NOT git restore), (3) git add + git commit the trashed state. Never attempt any git operation that undoes, unstages, restores, reverts, resets, cleans, checks out, switches, stashes, or rebases.

rules.name: block-git-commit-no-verify
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. git commit --no-verify bypasses pre-commit hooks that enforce quality gates, security scanning, and repo conventions. Agents must always allow hooks to run.

rules.name: block-git-commit-no-gpg-sign
rules.reason: NOT ALLOWED. git commit --no-gpg-sign bypasses commit signing policy. Agents must allow default signing.

rules.name: block-npm-test
rules.reason: STOP NOW. All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing environment. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-pytest
rules.reason: STOP NOW. All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing environment. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-vitest
rules.reason: STOP NOW. All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing environment. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-direnv-tests
rules.reason: STOP NOW. direnv exec bypasses justfile environment initialization. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-uv-pytest
rules.reason: STOP NOW. All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing environment. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-uv-vitest
rules.reason: STOP NOW. All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing environment. Use "just test" instead.

rules.name: block-just-typecheck
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Use "just test" which includes typecheck as a dependency. Do not bypass the full test suite.

rules.name: block-just-check
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Use "just test" which includes check as a dependency. Do not bypass the full test suite.

rules.name: block-npm-install-global
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Global package installation is BANNED. Use npx instead, or record in package.json and install via justfile recipe. Agents may not mutate global packages.

rules.name: block-pip-install-global
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Global package installation is BANNED. Use uvx instead, or record in pyproject.toml and install via justfile recipe. Agents may not mutate global packages.

rules.name: block-yarn-global
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Global package installation is BANNED. Use npx/uvx/bunx instead, or record in package.json and install via justfile recipe. Agents may not mutate global packages.

rules.name: block-npm-uninstall-global
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Global package removal is BANNED. Agents may not mutate global packages. Alert the user to manually uninstall if needed.

rules.name: block-pip-uninstall-global
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Global package removal is BANNED. Agents may not mutate global packages. Alert the user to manually uninstall if needed.

rules.name: block-just-private-recipe
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Private recipes (starting with _) are internal implementation details. Use public justfile recipes only.

rules.name: block-uvx-ruff
rules.reason: STOP NOW. QC tools must be routed through justfile recipes. Use "just lint-python" or "just fmt-python" instead.

rules.name: block-uvx-mypy
rules.reason: STOP NOW. QC tools must be routed through justfile recipes. Use "just mypy" instead.

rules.name: block-python-module
rules.reason: STOP NOW. Direct python -m execution is BANNED. Use "uv run" or "uvx" for Python CLI tools to ensure proper environment isolation.
